review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to anti-malware protection. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but a slight adjustment was made for consistency. The functions are technically accurate and relevant to the control.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of resource names, e.g., 'admission' instead of 'admission_controller' for brevity and alignment with existing patterns.
title: Anti-Malware Protection
step1_reasoning: The control 'Anti-Malware Protection' focuses on detecting and preventing malware threats. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that container images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment, which is akin to anti-malware scanning. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce policies that prevent the deployment of images with known vulnerabilities. Pod Security Standards can also enforce restrictions that reduce the risk of malware execution, such as running containers with restricted capabilities.
